title: Race-lap query ranker
summary: Pit calls are relative within a lap: teams decide against the field state, not in isolation.
rationale: Pit calls are relative within a lap: teams decide against the field state, not in isolation. Learning-to-rank frameworks explicitly optimize ordering inside query groups, which matches ROC AUC well when grouped by a common decision context.
implementation_hint: Train an `XGBRanker` or LightGBM ranker with query/group=`Year,Race,LapNumber` and binary labels `PitNextLap`. Keep existing numeric features, add within-group deltas/percentiles versus lap peers, then blend its scores with the current classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Better ordering among borderline same-lap candidates, especially around undercut/traffic windows; likely small CV gain and stronger ensemble diversity.
risk: Ranking loss is not identical to global AUC, and sparse positive labels per lap can make some groups weak; needs careful group construction and OOF evaluation.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
